page: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with four NVIDIA H100 GPUs based on the Hopper architecture, providing a total of 319 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, delivering 104 dedicated vCPUs across 52 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512).

The platform includes 936 GB of system memory, translating to 9.0 GB per core, and is equipped with 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance, particularly in memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth are rated as strong, placing in the top 25 percent. The system also achieves strong throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving.

Given the integration of four high-end GPUs and dedicated Intel Xeon processors with AMX support, this server is designed for intensive parallel computing tasks. It is highly suited for large language model inference, machine learning training, high-performance computing, and data-intensive database operations. The high resource density and specialized hardware profile represent a significant investment in compute power, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads that can fully exploit the parallel processing capabilities of the Hopper GPUs and the local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-performance parallel computing. It features four NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs with 319 GB of total VRAM, paired with 104 dedicated Intel Xeon 8481C vCPUs operating at 2.7 GHz. The system is provisioned with 936 GB of RAM and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth and CPU encryption, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. The hardware architecture supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel AMX and AVX-512, enhancing its efficiency for matrix operations. This server is optimized for demanding workloads including large language model inference, machine learning training, and data-intensive database operations where high-density GPU acceleration and fast local storage are critical.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 4 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 104 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 936 GB of RAM, and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for demanding AI and HPC workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-4g features 4 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 104 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 936 GB RAM, and 3216 GB NVMe SSD for high-performance AI workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring four NVIDIA H100 GPUs and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for high-performance parallel computing and machine learning.

104 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with AMX and AVX-512 support
936 GB system memory and 3216 GB local NVMe SSD storage
